plan_adherence_notes: Comparing the execute diff (8981acb4..58965972) against the recorded plan, the branch implements all four planned work items in backend/server.py and tests/test_bayes_market_api.py. Step 1 landed startup-scoped build metadata helpers and constants (`VERSION_ROUTE`, `BUILD_GIT_SHA`, `BUILD_TIMESTAMP`, `v1_version_payload()`) with `version` sourced from `ENGINE_CONFIG.version`. Step 2 landed GET-only routing for `/v1/version` with the existing 405 error shape and exposed the route from the root service index under `routes.health`. Step 3 updated the root index assertion and added direct route coverage for the `/v1/version` payload, including stable startup-scoped metadata across `reset_state()`. Step 4 added HTTP integration coverage for `GET /v1/version`. The only thing not visible in the execute diff is whether the implementer personally ran the planned tests during execution, but the implemented scope itself matches the plan closely and validation confirmed the tests now pass.
reason: The research supports the plan cleanly, and the plan is executed with no obvious scope drift: the branch adds the backend-only `/v1/version` endpoint, advertises it from the service index, and covers it with both direct dispatch and HTTP integration tests. Validation also ran `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_bayes_market_api.py'`, which passed all 313 tests, so I would approve this task.
